name.es: Mkate wa Ufuta (pan de sésamo suajili)
name.fr: Mkate wa Ufuta (pain au sésame swahili)

description.en: A firm, sesame-coated flatbread from the Swahili coast, its dough enriched with coconut milk and egg, then pan-fried until spotted brown on both sides. Zanzibar households serve it warm alongside soups, curries and stews for dipping.
description.de: Ein festes, mit Sesam bedecktes Fladenbrot von der Suaheli-Küste, der Teig mit Kokosmilch und Ei angereichert und beidseitig in der Pfanne gebraten bis gefleckt braun. In Sansibar wird es warm zu Suppen, Currys und Eintöpfen zum Eintunken serviert.
description.es: Un pan plano firme cubierto de sésamo de la costa suajili, con masa enriquecida con leche de coco y huevo, frito en sartén hasta dorarse por ambos lados. En Zanzíbar se sirve tibio junto a sopas, currys y guisos para mojar.
description.fr: Un pain plat ferme couvert de sésame de la côte swahilie, la pâte enrichie au lait de coco et à l'oeuf, cuite à la poêle jusqu'à brunir des deux côtés. À Zanzibar, on le sert tiède avec des soupes, currys et ragoûts pour tremper.

origin.region: Swahili coast (Zanzibar, coastal Kenya and Tanzania)
origin.note: Reflects the Swahili coast's Indian Ocean trade history, where coconut, sesame and wheat converge in one griddle bread.

ingredients.note: for coating both sides before cooking

keeping.note: Best served warm right off the pan; leftovers reheat well in a dry skillet.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Whisk flour, yeast and salt, then stir in coconut milk and egg to form a dense, firm dough, not a spongy one.

method.title: Knead
method.summary: Knead 5 to 7 minutes until smooth, adding a little more coconut milk if the dough is too dry.

method.title: Rest
method.summary: Cover with a damp towel and let rest in a warm spot 30 to 60 minutes until slightly puffier.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Divide into 6 balls and pat each into a round about 16 cm wide and 0.5 to 1 cm thick.

method.title: Coat
method.summary: Oil the tops generously and press sesame seeds into both sides.

method.title: Cook
method.summary: Cook sesame-side down in a hot skillet 1.5 to 2 minutes until browned, flip, oil and season the other side, and cook 1 to 2 minutes more.

method.title: Serve
method.summary: Wipe the pan between breads so the seeds do not scorch; serve warm alongside soups or stews.

source.adaptation: Coconut milk's contribution to hydration estimated at 65% of its weight and its fat at 24%, since no standard conversion is given for it; cup/tsp/tbsp quantities converted to grams.
